channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The US is killing someone by firing squad for the 1st time in 15 years. Here's a look at the history
item.description: It was a punishment for mutiny in colonial times, a way to discourage desertion during the Civil War and a dose of frontier justice in the Old West. In modern times, some consider it a more humane alternative to lethal injection. The firing squad has a long and thorny history in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mötley Crüe postpones Las Vegas residency due to Vince Neil's health
item.description: The band said the postponement was due to a required medical procedure advised by Neil's doctors, the band said.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio lawmaker proposes gym membership tax credit
item.description: “They can get a move away from the pharmaceuticals and focus a little bit more on just lifestyle changes,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Republican introduces censure resolution against Al Green
item.description: The House will vote this week on a resolution from Rep. Dan Newhouse (R-Wash.) to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his outburst at President Trump’s speech to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Mötley Crüe postpones Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health
item.description: Mötley Crüe announced they are postponing their upcoming Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, Fox 5

item.title: Bill to ban Planned Parenthood from teaching health in Utah schools advances
item.description: Opponents say the language of the bill is broad enough that it could block large hospital system employees from teaching those school health programs.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Aerts, NewsNation

item.title: Fired Los Angeles Fire Chief Kristin Crowley has a new job
item.description: Crowley, the first woman to serve as LAFD's Chief, was fired by Mayor Karen Bass on Feb. 21 after almost three years in the role.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Sony Honda EV puts movies on the dashboard
item.description: Get ready for your car to have even more personality. Recently, I checked out AFEELA, a new EV from Sony and Honda. It’s half car, half entertainment hub - and yes, it’s real. Right now it's on display at a pop-up shop at Westfield Century City.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Mysterious affliction causing endangered Florida fish to spin and die is back
item.description: Following a roughly nine-month lull, there have been 22 reports of the odd spinning behavior involving sawfish in waters around the Florida Keys since December.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump admin plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs: internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning an “aggressive” reorganization that includes cutting 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care for retired military members, according to an internal memo obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: California congressmember pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs, according to internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning an “aggressive” reorganization that includes cutting 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care for retired military members.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Boy reportedly kidnapped from Atlanta 7 years ago found in Colorado; 2 arrested
item.description: A 14-year-old boy who went missing seven years ago has been located, some 1,400 miles from where he was allegedly abducted.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, KTLA 5

item.title: Avalanche buries at least 3 skiers near Anchorage, Alaska officials say
item.description: Alaska State Troopers say three skiers who had flown by helicopter to a remote mountain range were swept away by an avalanche and were likely killed under the weight of snow nearly as deep as a 10-story building.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US charges Chinese hackers, government officials in broad cyberespionage campaign
item.description: Twelve Chinese nationals, including mercenary hackers, law enforcement officers and employees of a private hacking company, have been charged in connection with global cyberespionage campaigns targeting dissidents, news organizations, U.S. agencies and universities.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration moves to drop Idaho emergency abortion case with national implications
item.description: The Trump administration is dropping an emergency abortion case in Idaho in one of the administration’s first moves on the issue since President Donald Trump's second term began.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst And Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Watch: Fishermen rush to save 900-pound dolphin that landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 900 pounds appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope increases physical therapy, marks start of Lent from hospital
item.description: During the morning he participated in an Ash Wednesday celebration, receiving ashes and Holy Communion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: These nuns advocated for immigrant rights for 40 years
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — At age 90 and 95, this Catholic nun dynamic duo have been “peacefully and respectfully” fighting for immigrant rights for over 40 years. And they never take no for an answer.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elissa Slotkin assails Trump's early actions, offers Democrats a way to fight back
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — First-term Michigan Sen. Elissa Slotkin accused President Donald Trump of driving up costs while pushing for an “unprecedented giveaway to his billionaire friends” in Tuesday night's Democratic response to his first joint congressional address of his second term.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Powerful US storms kill 2 and bring threats from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms killed two people in Mississippi, tore the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma and threatened more communities across the nation Tuesday with wide-ranging weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Florida man swallows $769K in stolen jewelry from Tiffany & Co.: police
item.description: A man was accused of stealing hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of jewelry from a Tiffany & Co. store in Orlando and swallowing the evidence.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Couple arrested after allegedly throwing coffee at airline employees, forcing way onto Cancun flight
item.description: A Florida couple were arrested after allegedly assaulting employees at an American Airlines gate at Miami International Airport Sunday when they were denied boarding for arriving late.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Pope Francis no longer requires mechanical ventilation after respiratory crisis
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis had stabilized enough Tuesday after two respiratory crises to be taken off noninvasive mechanical ventilation and receive just high flows of supplemental oxygen, as he continued his more than two-week long fight against double pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, Fox 5

item.title: 2 leaders of a group suspected of smuggling 20,000 immigrants are arrested in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested two alleged leaders of a criminal organization suspected of smuggling 20,000 people from Guatemala to the U.S. and charging each person as much as $18,000 to get them into the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ariana Grande goes full Glinda, 'Wicked' bestie Erivo does Elphaba proud in green on Oscars carpet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ariana Grande did her Glinda proud in a pale pink stunner, and her “Wicked” bestie Cynthia Erivo honored Elphaba in deep forest green on the Oscars carpet as high glam took over Sunday. Others went for bold red, classic black and pops of pink.
item.dc:creator: LEANNE ITALIE and BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Magnitude 4.5 earthquake strikes in Washington state
item.description: A “notable” earthquake registering at a preliminary magnitude of 4.5 struck Washington State on Monday morning, according to the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's next first speech to Congress is bound to have little resemblance to his last first one
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation will hear a new president sing a far different tune in his prime-time address before Congress on Tuesday night. Some Americans will lustily sing along. Others will plug their ears.
item.dc:creator: CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Oscars 2025: How to watch, and when the show begins
item.description: The Oscars themselves will start at 7 p.m. EST but red carpet coverage has already kicked off on ABC and Hulu, E!, and The Associated Press. The official Oscars preshow on ABC and Hulu will begin at 6:30 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Photos: The 2025 Oscars Red Carpet in Los Angeles
item.description: Hollywood's biggest stars and most talented artists are set to be honored Sunday evening during the 97th Academy Awards held at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood. But before the awards ceremony itself begins, the stars are walking the iconic Red Carpet where fashion takes center stage.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Enabling this phone setting reduces anxiety, Gen Z says
item.description: It can create "a buffer against unwanted communications and potential social pressures," a psychotherapist said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Nevada man pleads guilty to extorting Instagram users, influencers
item.description: A Las Vegas man has pleaded guilty to extorting and threatening Instagram users and influencers, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, WGN 9
